title: Word Search II
pattern: Trie + Backtracking â€” DFS with Prefix Pruning
coreIdea: Find all words from a given list that can be formed on a character grid via adjacent cells. Building a trie of the word list allows DFS from each grid cell to prune paths early whenever the current path is not a valid prefix of any word.
recognitionCues: multiple target words given (not just one) to search in a grid
brute-force DFS per word would be too slow, hinting at trie-based optimization
approach: Build a trie from all target words. For each cell in the grid, run DFS, at each step checking if the current path forms a valid prefix in the trie (using trie traversal alongside DFS instead of separate string matching). If a complete word is found (end-of-word marker reached), add it to results. Backtrack after each cell to try other paths.
timeComplexity: O(rows * cols * 4^L) bounded further by trie pruning
spaceComplexity: O(total characters in word list) for trie, O(L) recursion depth
